title: campanula rotundifolia subvar. pinifolia
intro: Campanula rotundifolia subvar. pinifolia, also known as the Hairy Bellflower or Scottish Bluebell, is a plant species belonging to the bellflower family (Campanulaceae) and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, sore throats, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Campanula rotundifolia subvar. pinifolia include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
